title: Oplismenus parvifolius
taxonomy: Oplismenus parvifolius is a species of grass within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the true grasses. It belongs to the subfamily Chloridoideae and is part of the tribe Ehrhartoideae. Further classification places Oplismenus parvifolius within the genus Oplismenus. The genus Oplismenus is native to tropical and subtropical regions, with species often found in grasslands and open woods. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary history and relationships within the plant kingdom.
uses: Oplismenus parvifolius, also known as small-leaved basket grass, is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research has suggested that extracts from Oplismenus parvifolius may possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. However, more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. In traditional medicine, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory issues. intro: Oplismenus parvifolius, also known as small-leaved grass-leaved false dandelion or small-leaved false dandelion, is a plant species in the family Poaceae.
